title: Rich, Spicy Thai Pork Moo Ping
description: A peppery and creamy Thai dish featuring crispy bacon, flavorful pork, and fragrant rice. Perfect for a special occasion or a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Cook the pork shoulder in a large skillet over medium-high heat until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Transfer the pork to a plate and set aside.
In the same skillet, add the bacon and cook until crispy, about 3-4 minutes.
Remove the bacon from the skillet and set aside with the pork.
In the same skillet, add the rice flour and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the coconut milk, fish sauce, soy sauce, brown sugar, garlic, ginger, and Thai red chilies to the skillet.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook until thickened, about 5-7 minutes.
Stir in the cooked pork and bacon, and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve the moo ping hot, garnished with chopped cilantro and a squeeze of lime juice.
Serve with steamed rice or roti.
